channel.title: NBC Sports Fantasy Football News
channel.description: Latest player news and analysis for fantasy football from NBC Sports.

item.title: Packers restructured OG Aaron Banks’ contract.
item.description: According to ESPN’s Jeremy Fowler, this “simple restructure” will reduce Banks’ 2026 cap hit down to $24.8 million. Banks, who was drafted in the second round by the 49ers back in 2021, signed a four-year contract with the Packers last offseason and is under contract through 2028. The five-year vet started 14 of the 15 games he appeared in for the Packers in 2025.

item.title: CBS Sports’ Jonathan Jones says “it would be a surprise” if the Jaguars traded Brian Thomas Jr.
item.description: There has been nothing to suggest that the Jaguars would be open to dealing Thomas, whose down 2025 season had many talking about a potential trade, especially with Parker Washington emerging and Jakobi Meyers now in town. That said, Jones said the Jaguars “value the 2024 first rounder” and believes trading Thomas isn’t on the table at this time. Thomas erupted for 87-1,282-10 as a rookie but as his numbers fall off to a pedestrian 48-707-2 in 14 games last season. A bounce back in 2026 would be nice to see, but he’ll again face stiff target competition.

item.title: Cleveland.com’s Mary Kay Cabot reports the Browns “are expected to be among the multiple teams interested” in Mike Evans.
item.description: Evans will likely look to join a team he believes can contend for a title in 2026, but his relationship with Browns head coach Todd Monken could make Cleveland a sleeper destination. Evans spent three seasons with Monken (2016-2018) and had three-straight 1,000-yard seasons under him. The 173 targets Evans saw in 2016 led all receivers that season and remain the most he has ever seen in his career. It would be legitimately surprising if Evans decided to spend what could be the final season or two of his career in Cleveland, but we can’t blame them for wanting to see if there’s any interest in the soon-to-be 33-year-old receiver.

item.title: The Athletic’s Dianna Russini reports Travis Kelce “appears motivated to return” for next season.
item.description: Russini says this is “based on conversations with several teams.” She also adds that the Chiefs “remain very much in the mix to re-sign” Kelce, who will be a free agent when the new league year begins this week. Kelce, 36, has been rumored to be considering a return next season over retirement, and this is yet another report that suggests he’ll likely play in 2026. While he will be free to speak with other teams, it would be surprising to see him play for anyone other than the Chiefs if he does return for another season.

item.title: Cardinals re-signed K Chad Ryland to a one-year contract.
item.description: Ryland has spent the last two seasons with the Cardinals, converting on 81.5 percent of his field goal attempts over that span while going 10-of-14 from 50-plus yards. The 26-year-old kicker was 6-of-10 from that distance in 2025 and also made all 36 of his extra point attempts. The decision to bring back Ryland was a no-brainer for head coach Mike LaFleur, although he’ll likely face competition for the kicking job after the Cards brough in Josh Karty last December.

item.title: Cardinals re-signed P Blake Gillikin.
item.description: A back injury caused Gillikin to miss 12 games last season, but over the five games he did appear in, Gillikin led all punters with 51.7 yards per punt while pinning his opponent inside the 20-yard line on eight of his 22 punts. Gillikin can earn up to $2.65 million on his new deal.

item.title: Colts signed K Blake Grupe to a one-year, $1.4 million contract.
item.description: Grupe appeared in only five games for the Colts in 2025 but went a perfect 11-for-11 with the team. He was signed in December after making only 18 of 26 attempts with the Saints to start the year. His efforts to end the season earn him a chance to prove his worth on the modest deal. Spencer Shrader remains under team control, setting up a potential kicker battle in camp and preseason.

item.title: Senior NFL Insider Josina Anderson reports “eight to nine teams” are interested in impending free agent CB James Pierre.
item.description: The six-year veteran has made only 13 professional starts with the Steelers but is expected to draw significant interest in the free agent market if not signed by Monday, Senior NFL Insider Josina Anderson reports. This comes on the heels of the expectation that numerous teams will be looking to the free agent pool for help at cornerback in the coming cycle, a notion that could spark rapid fire signings in the secondary. Pierre was a mainstay on special teams in Pittsburgh and contributed 614 defensive snaps the previous two seasons.

item.title: NFL Network’s Mike Garafolo reports the Rams have made “significant progress” on an extension for CB Trent McDuffie.
item.description: Garafolo reports the team hopes to sign McDuffie to a deal “in the near future,” adding that he’ll likely become the highest-paid corner in the league when all is said and done. McDuffie, 25, was acquired by the Rams last week in exchange for a haul of picks that included the Rams 2026 first-round pick. The fourth-year corner totaled 63 tackles, one interception, and seven pass breakups in 13 games last season and was named a First-team All-Pro in 2023 and a Second-team All-Pro in 2024.

item.title: The Athletic reports Travis Kelce’s representatives are “expected to talk to multiple teams” when the legal tampering period begins this week.
item.description: Kelce is set to be a free agent when the new league year begins, and while it sounds like he will be returning for a 14th season, he will likely shop his services to other teams before deciding whether to re-up with the Chiefs. While it would still be surprising to see Kelce play for another team in what could be his final season, there will likely be plenty of teams lining up to speak with the future Hall of Famer. Kelce caught 76 passes for 851 yards and five touchdowns last season on 108 targets. While he’s taken a step back in recent years, his production relative to his position continues to come at a high level. Rumors of potential suitors are sure to emerge once the league’s legal tampering period opens on Monday, which could make for an interesting start to free agency.
